108.status: Exited in the seventh inning on May 23 after experiencing calf tightness. The issue has bothered him throughout the season, though he hopes he'll be able to play through it and was held out as a precaution on May 24.
Manager Kurt Suzuki said Pomeranz’s placement on the IL was the Angels being “cautious.” Pomeranz threw a second bullpen session on May 21 and faced hitters in a live session on May 23.
Same knee that sidelined Moncada last season. Getting MRI on May 22 to know exactly what is going on.
Left April 29 outing before the start of the third inning. Targeting to start throwing program in late May/early June with a return date shortly after the All-Star break.
Made four rehab appearances before being shut down. MRI came back clean May 15. Eligible to return from the 60-day IL May 26. Rehabbing at the club's complex in Arizona.
Exited in the fifth inning on May 6 after injuring himself while trying to beat out an infield single. Said he felt a pop and won't be able to put pressure on his foot until early June.
Was evaluated by Dr. Keith Meister in Texas and underwent season-ending ligament and flexor tendon repair surgery, the club announced April 8.
Not going to play in 2026 and had the last year of his contract deferred over several years. Was placed on 60-day IL on March 24.

110.status: Scratched from lineup on May 24. Back was feeling better on May 25, though he remained out. Manager Craig Albernaz doesn't believe it will be an IL situation.

Began rehab assignment with Double-A Chesapeake on May 9 and went 1-for-6 in two games. Moved to Triple-A Norfolk on May 12 and went 10-for-41 with a double, a home run and six RBIs in first 10 games.
Exited May 7 start in Miami due to left forearm discomfort. Was examined by Dr. Keith Meister and received cortisone shot on May 19.
Was evaluated on May 19 due to mild shoulder symptoms. Continuing to discuss treatment options. No clear timeline for return.
Injury was sustained in days after April 18 start in Cleveland. Joined team in St. Petersburg, Fla., for series vs. Rays on May 18.
Imaging came back clean. Took anti-inflammatory medication. Began treatment May 4. Threw for first time on May 15 at Nationals Park in Washington, then again May 16.
Underwent season-ending Tommy John surgery on May 13. Expected to recover in time to return during the early part of the 2027 season.
Scratched from lineup ahead of May 12 game vs. the Yankees after feeling discomfort during pregame work. Underwent an MRI and was placed on IL the next day.
Got second opinion with Dr. Neal ElAttrache in Los Angeles on April 20. Has inflammation in shoulder joint. Felt soreness coming out of March 31 outing.
Exited April 11 game vs. Giants after stumbling between first and second and sliding awkwardly into the bag on a double. Broke fourth metatarsal on left foot. Expected to miss substantial time.
Exited 2026 debut on March 31 due to right elbow discomfort. After getting imaging and a second opinion, underwent Tommy John surgery on April 8.
Underwent surgery to repair torn rotator cuff and torn labrum last Aug. 19. Threw for first time March 3, tossing 25 pitches from 45 feet on flat ground.

111.status: Threw his fourth side session in 10 days on May 23. The next step is live batting practice at Fenway on May 26.
Underwent surgery in Philadelphia from Dr. William Meyers. Per Red Sox interim manager Chad Tracy, he will be "out a while."
Not swinging yet, but has advanced to rotational exercises.

111.expectedReturn: Mid to late June at earliest
111.status: Threw another side session on May 22. Hopes to face hitters soon.
Continues to throw off flat ground.
Will soon start forearm strengthening exercises with an eye toward throwing by mid-June.

111.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament right elbow/right flexor pronator strain
111.status: Making 75 throws out to 105 feet three days a week, and 30 throws at 60 feet three days a week while throwing changeups those days. Targeting mound work in late June.

112.status: Left knee meniscectomy (May 7). Threw off mound May 16 and 19. Simulated multiple innings off mound on May 22. Next step is live BP on May 26 in Pittsburgh.
Worked out on the field on May 25, but is not ready to resume full baseball activities.
Exited start against Brewers on May 20 after throwing one pitch in fourth inning due to blister issue. Tested things out off mound on May 24 and was placed on IL after the workout.
Felt some discomfort in April 23 outing. Further testing showed a flexor strain; timetable estimated to be around eight weeks. Transferred to 60-day IL on May 17.
Had setback (stress reaction near triceps/shoulder) after May 7 bullpen session, per manager Craig Counsell. Shut down for a month and moved rehab to Arizona. Transferred to 60-day IL on May 17.

Felt discomfort in recent bullpen session. Checkup with Dr. Keith Meister (who did Steele's UCL revision repair on April 18, 2025) showed a flexor strain. Shut down from throwing until asymptomatic, and will be rechecked in a month.

112.playerName: Jaxon Wiggins (Cubs' No. 3 prospect, No. 88 overall)
112.status: Last pitched on April 4 for Iowa. Moved to Minor League IL out of caution. Has resumed playing catch, per president of baseball operations Jed Hoyer.
Underwent reconstruction of ulnar collateral ligament with an internal brace April 20 via Dr. Keith Meister. Initial timetable for return is 12-14 months.
Underwent a right UCL revision repair with an internal brace on April 16. Timetable for return is estimated around 15-16 months.
Underwent a right patellar tendon debridement on Feb. 24. Could miss months while recovering.
Underwent surgery to repair a torn right UCL and flexor tendon last October. Signed a two-year deal with Chicago on Feb. 16 with an eye toward contributing in 2027.

113.status: Slated to have a "pretty active day" on May 25, including more intense throwing. Expected to face hitters in live BP on May 27.
Will head to Arizona to rehab.
MRI revealed a Grade 1 strain of Trevino’s left hamstring behind his knee. Manager Terry Francona said Trevino could be back with the team in three to four weeks, though there is no exact timetable.
Throwing out to 150 feet in Arizona. First bullpen session scheduled for May 26. If all goes well, will rejoin the team on May 29 to continue his rehab.
MRI exam showed a Grade 2 strain, with estimated time away to be four to eight weeks. Starting throwing from his knees on May 8.
Went to Goodyear, Ariz., to begin rehab process on May 5. Moved to the 60-day IL on May 6, but there was no setback.

114.status: Team awaiting results of MRI exam from May 25. Felt "a little sore" following outing on May 20, per manager Stephen Vogt.
Doing baseball activities and hitting in cage. Initial timeline was four to eight weeks. Stephen Vogt noted May 8 it would be "a little bit longer" after "a little snag."

115.status: Exited in the first inning May 25 after a hit-by-pitch on his right hand. X-rays were negative, with Rumfield set for further testing May 26.
Flew back to Denver following his start on May 24 to undergo an MRI on his elbow.
Rejoined the team in Los Angeles on May 25 for a medical checkup after throwing five innings in a rehab start with Triple-A Albuquerque on May 24.
Did not travel to Los Angeles; returned home to Denver to continue rehab and await an upcoming medical evaluation.
Still not throwing and did not travel to Los Angeles. Is continuing a rehab program in Denver.
Did not travel to Los Angeles. Currently staying back in Denver to receive localized treatment; has not resumed baseball activities.
Making steady progress in his overall rehabilitation program. Played catch on the field pregame May 25.
Played catch on May 24; noted that he felt good. Showing consistent progress in his shoulder rehabilitation program and was scheduled to play catch again May 25.

115.expectedReturn: To be determined after he is cleared for game action
115.status: Continued throwing program and playing catch; officially scheduled to throw a live bullpen session at the end of the month.
Logged his eighth rehab appearance on May 23 and remains scheduled to make another rehab outing soon.
Did not travel to Los Angeles; remained in Denver where he is continuing to progress in his running rehab program.

115.playerName: Ethan Holliday (Rockies' No. 1 prospect, No. 17 overall)
115.status: Has not been in the Single-A Fresno lineup since May 20 due to a left foot injury that is undergoing further evaluation.
Dr. Keith Meister performed Tommy John surgery with an internal brace March 20 in Dallas. Petit, a Rule 5 Draft pick from the Tigers, will miss the season.
Underwent ligament transfer Tommy John surgery and internal brace procedure on March 4.
There is no return in sight for Bryant, who has had a long and painful time since signing with the Rockies before the 2022 season.

116.status: Threw an indoor bullpen session on May 23 in Baltimore. Manager A.J. Hinch said he hopes Skubal can throw live batting practice during Tigers’ series at Comerica Park on May 26-28.
After throwing a bullpen in Baltimore, will throw live BP during the Tigers’ May 26-28 series at Comerica Park.
Rejoined the club in Baltimore on May 22 to take four days off from swinging a bat. Will attempt to re-enter the batting cage on May 26 in Detroit. Running and playing defense at full strength.
Exited Detroit’s May 22 game at Baltimore in the fifth inning following a pitch that resulted in Tyler O’Neill’s flyout to right. Hinch said Hurter complained of back soreness upon a mound visit.

Had a cortisone injection after he reported shoulder soreness following a relief outing in which he allowed one run in two-thirds of an inning on May 21. Was placed on the 15-day IL before Detroit’s May 22 game in Baltimore.
Was able to throw a bullpen session on May 21. Left game on May 15 after taking a line drive off right forearm. Initial tests indicated no fracture.
Threw latest bullpen session on May 22.

Threw a 21-pitch inning for Triple-A Toledo on May 20 with a home run allowed, a walk and a strikeout. Fastball averaged 95.8 mph, topping out at 96.9. Scheduled to pitch again May 21 for Toledo before being evaluated.
Cleared for more baseball activity this week, including defensive work. Left game May 9 after crashing into the wall. Diagnosed with AC joint sprain but no structural damage.
Began a throwing progression on May 14 after receiving a cortisone injection in mid-April.

116.injury: Concussion, fractured left radius bone in arm
116.status: Completing rehab daily after undergoing surgery on April 13 to repair radial fracture in left forearm following an outfield collision on April 9.

117.status: After homering twice May 23 in his third Minor League rehab game at Double-A Corpus Christi, Trammell went 0-for-4 with a strikeout on May 24.
Threw one scoreless inning, striking out two, in his seventh Minor League rehab outing on May 24 at Double-A Corpus Christi. He's getting close to rejoining the big league club.
Went 0-for-2 with a two strikeouts in his first Minor League rehab game May 24 with Double-A Corpus Christi and fouled a ball off his foot that will keep out of action a couple of days.

Threw two scoreless innings (35 pitches), allowing one hit and striking out five batters, in his first Minor League rehab start May 24 for Double-A Corpus Christi. He will make his next start this weekend at Triple-A.
Was scratched before his scheduled start against the Twins in Minneapolis on May 19 and remains a no-throw while battling inflammation.
Exited May 16 game with pain in left side after a swing and will head to Florida on May 25 to continue his rehab.
Has begun running and swinging, building toward hitting off a tee next.
Was still experiencing soreness and was not throwing, manager Joe Espada said May 14. He first experienced soreness during his throwing program on May 8 and was sent home to Houston to see doctors.
Underwent peroneus brevis tendon repair surgery on his left ankle May 11. He will be out for the remainder of the 2026 season.

117.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament in right elbow
117.status: Has progressed to throwing in the bullpen in West Palm Beach after undergoing Tommy John surgery to repair the ulnar collateral ligament with an internal brace as well as a repair of the flexor tendon in his forearm on June 6, 2025.

117.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament in right elbow
117.status: Has progressed to throwing in the bullpen in West Palm Beach after undergoing Tommy John surgery to repair the ulnar collateral ligament in his right elbow on May 23, 2025.

117.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament in left elbow
117.status: Underwent Tommy John surgery on Sept. 23, 2025.

118.status: Threw 4 1/3 innings (68 pitches) in first outing with Triple-A Omaha May 23, but did not recover well with more stiffness. Shut down for two or three days to see if symptoms resolve.
Patellar tendinitis flared up during May 15 outing in St. Louis. Received injection May 18 to help symptoms. Threw a bullpen session May 25 and is scheduled for a live BP in the coming days.
Felt more soreness than typical after May 14 start against White Sox, but severity concern is low. Began playing catch May 22 and started full throwing program May 23.

Underwent another elbow surgery May 22 to try to help continued problems with soreness and inflammation. Return-to-play timeline is unknown so far and will depend on how McArthur recovers in the short-term.
Suffered injury during first rehab outing on May 6 and will be shut down for three weeks. Returned from rehab assignment May 8.
Underwent left labral repair surgery on April 28, ending his season.
After missing 2025 with shoulder issues, underwent right labral surgery in October. Rehabbing in Surprise, Ariz., during Spring Training but likely out for year.

119.injury: Hit by pitch on right wrist
119.status: Initial X-rays came back negative. The Dodgers remain hopeful that he can avoid an IL stint, though he is expected to miss up to four games.
Has resumed playing catch after a small setback. He's missed enough time that he should need a rehab assignment before returning to the Dodgers.
Set to begin a throwing progression on May 26.
Underwent the NanoNeedle Scope procedure to remove loose bodies from his elbow on May 19, which should allow for a faster return.

119.injury: Recovery from right shoulder surgery, lower back injury
119.status: Injured his lower back while on a rehab assignment and surgery could be on the table, per general manager Brandon Gomes.
Has resumed throwing and could return around three weeks after landing on the IL if all goes as planned.
Rehabbing at the Dodgers' complex in Arizona.
Working through the early stages of a throwing program at the Dodgers' complex in Arizona.
An MRI revealed inflammation, but no structural damage. The hope is that he won't miss much longer than the minimum 15 days.
Rehabbing at the Dodgers' complex in Arizona.
Underwent Tommy John surgery in June 2025 and could be a second-half option for the Dodgers.
Rehabbing at the Dodgers' complex in Arizona.
Working through a throwing program at the Dodgers' complex in Arizona.

120.status: Continuing his throwing program at the Nationals' training complex in West Palm Beach, Fla., and threw a bullpen session on May 22. Transferred to 60-day IL on May 25
Left May 23 game in the sixth inning with shoulder tightness and was diagnosed with a shoulder strain after a May 24 MRI. He’ll undergo treatments and strengthening to manage injury.
Continuing throwing progression at Nationals' training complex. Threw on May 22 and is set to throw May 25.
Rehab assignment transferred to Triple-A Rochester on May 17. Will remain with team after making two appearances in first week of assignment
Threw a 15-pitch live batting practice at the Nationals’ training complex in West Palm Beach, Fla., on May 15. Threw a 22-pitch live BP on May 20. Scheduled to throw another one on May 25.
Throwing bullpen sessions at the Nationals’ training complex in West Palm Beach, Fla., including 30 pitches on May 20 and 31 pitches on May 23.
Continuing his throwing progression at the Nationals' training complex in West Palm Beach, Fla.
Continuing his throwing progression at the Nationals' training complex in West Palm Beach, Fla.
Underwent Tommy John surgery and internal brace procedure on April 20 to repair flexor tendon and UCL..

121.status: Hurt the hip while running the bases in the sixth inning May 25. Will undergo an MRI May 26 and will likely go on the IL, per Carlos Mendoza.
Missed second consecutive game because of flu-like symptoms. Developed a fever Sunday night and was too weak to play late Monday afternoon.
Has started baseball activities -- fielding drills, running sprints and swinging the bat in the indoor cages. It is not known when he will begin a rehab assignment.
Originally expected to miss six to eight weeks but may be back sooner than expected, as he is already doing catching drills and hitting in the batting cage.
Manager Carlos Mendoza said Polanco is taking live at-bats in Port St. Lucie and could get a Minor League rehab assignment soon.

Senga threw 64 pitches over 3 1/3 innings and reached 97 mph in his first rehab start for Single-A St. Lucie on May 22. Manager Carlos Mendoza said Senga will return to New York for further evaluation. “It feels good, but I haven’t heard much,” Mendoza said.

121.injury: Left lat surgery, left hip discomfort
121.status: Restarted rehab assignment May 15 after leaving previous one due to a setback. Was expected to pitch May 23 for Triple-A Syracuse, but that game was rained out. Minter should pitch on May 24, setting up his likely activation two or three days later.
Began a rehab assignment May 15, almost exactly one month after surgery, and advanced to Triple-A Syracuse on May 19. Is fully healthy but still needs work on his timing at the plate.

Fractured his leg on a comebacker May 15. Will need 6-8 weeks to heal and another six or so for a Spring Training-style build-up. That should take him well past the All-Star break in a best-case scenario.

Received an injection April 30 but was still experiencing symptoms as of a May 12 update. Is consulting with outside doctors, though surgery is not on the table. Timeline will remain fluid until symptoms subside.
Fractured his thumb sliding into first base in a May 2 loss to the Angels. Will miss 6-8 weeks.
Fractured a rib during a March 5 game against the Nationals. Will need 4-8 weeks to recover, after which he can commence his buildup.
Will miss all of 2026 recovering from surgery but is under team control through 2029.
Will miss all of 2026 recovering from surgery but is under team control through 2027.
Will miss all of 2026 recovering from surgery but is under team control through 2029.

133.status: Will undergo testing May 26 and will "most likely" be placed on the IL after decreased velocity in two straight starts.
Began a hitting progression May 25 by hitting off a tee. Is playing catch and will start ground-ball work the week of May 25. No timetable for return.
Took batting practice and performed infield drills on May 25 at Sutter Health Park. No timetable for return.
Pulled from rehab assignment after sustaining a "high-grade" hamstring strain on May 19. Will receive a platelet-rich plasma injection May 26 and will be reassessed after the All-Star break.
Shut down from throwing for a minimum of three weeks, beginning May 18. Received an ultrasound guided cortisone injection on May 19.
Underwent season-ending left hip surgery on May 19. Will rehab the rest of this season in hopes of being ready for 2027 Spring Training.

134.status: Made rehab start May 23 with Triple-A Indianapolis, allowing 3 ER and 2 hits over 4 1/3 IP while striking out 6. GM Ben Cherington said that Jones will throw a bullpen in Pittsburgh on May 26 before the team determines next steps.
Suffered apparent injury on May 9 in San Francisco. Foot began swelling during flight to Pittsburgh on May 10. Will see doctor on May 28 for follow-up assessment.
Has resumed all baseball activities and threw a bullpen on May 13.

135.status: Fouled a ball off his toe on May 5, sustaining what the team said was a small fracture. Making notable progress -- is swinging pain-free and catching bullpens.
Landed on the IL after his start on May 1. Began a throwing program after two weeks of rest and recovery. Could throw live BP in the coming days.
Underwent surgery in October 2024. Was hopeful for an early-season return but experienced a setback after making one start in Spring Training. Could be nearing the start of a throwing progression.
Expected to pitch again this season, after MRI revealed a flexor strain. Could be nearing the start of a throwing progression.
Placed on the injured list one day after working two scoreless innings in relief at Milwaukee. Began a throwing program on May 23. Could return as a reliever.
Underwent UCL internal brace surgery in April 2025. Began a rehab stint on May 7. Had his rehab transferred to Double-A San Antonio on May 17. Is building up as a starter.
Experienced concussion symptoms after a fastball off the chin on April 18. Initially cleared protocols, but symptoms arose in the days afterward. Has not resumed baseball activity.
Underwent surgery to repair the flexor tendon in his right elbow on March 17 and will miss the 2026 season.

Underwent an internal brace procedure on his right elbow on Oct. 29, which comes with a recovery timetable of 12-15 months. Will miss the 2026 season and was placed on the restricted list on March 25.

136.status: Reportedly began baseball activity with light catch on May 25, but will likely remain in Arizona rehabbing. MRI revealed Grade 1 strain.More »
Rejoined big league club in West Sacramento on May 25, after making two Minors rehab outings. Club wanted to see how he bounced back before activation.
Received a PRP injection for a groin strain, as well as a different injection for an adductor injury. Mariners hope he can return to baseball activities by early June. (last updated: May 17) More »
Injured left groin during a rehab assignment and was shut down on April 18. Is now targeting mid-June to begin fresh rehab assignment.
Recent X-rays showed positive healing, which would lead him to ramp up baseball activity in early June.
Has begun throwing again after experiencing setback during early stages and is making progress.
Underwent surgery to repair his right ulnar collateral ligament in January, and will be out all season.More »

137.status: Allowed one unearned run over 3 1/3 innings in his first rehab start for Triple-A Sacramento on May 22. Will throw a bullpen session this week before the Giants determine his next steps.
Placed on the IL after missing two games with back spasms. Lee's absence is expected to be "minimal, unless something crazy pops up," per manager Tony Vitello. Eligible to return on May 29.
Received a PRP injection on May 19 in San Francisco. Will be shut down from baseball activity for the next week.
Had his rehab assignment transferred to Triple-A Sacramento on May 13. Has tossed three scoreless innings in his first three appearances for the River Cats.
Began rehab assignment with Triple-A Tacoma on May 19, pitching a scoreless inning on 13 pitches.
Continuing his swing progression and increasing his baseball activity.
Underwent a second surgery to repair an axillary artery in his upper right arm on April 17, which will sideline him for five to six months.
Underwent season-ending Tommy John surgery with Dr. Keith Meister on March 25.
Expected to miss 12-14 months after undergoing Tommy John surgery on Sept. 24, 2025.
Expected to miss the entire 2026 season after undergoing Tommy John surgery in November 2025.

138.status: Had three plate appearances as a left fielder on May 21 with Triple-A Memphis. Will continue his rehab assignment with Double-A Springfield on May 26.
Urías had progressed to dry swings and his plyo program by May 25 and could start throwing program soon.

Scratched May 21 after feeling like he was unable to swing at 100 percent, then placed on IL. Imaging showed inflammation and a bit of a strain, but no structural damage. Will see doctor again May 26.

139.status: Felt his hamstring “grab” while running to first base on an infield single May 22. Did not feel better running first to third and exited the game. MRI revealed a Grade 1 strain that will sideline him for 6-8 weeks.

Felt a spasm in his back while fielding a ground ball in his last appearance May 16. Rays waited until May 20 to place him on the IL, hoping he will need only the minimum stint. Feeling "better" as of May 24.
Scratched from May 16 lineup and placed on the IL one day later. Manager Kevin Cash said he could require 6-8 weeks of recovery after surgery.
Felt his back "lock up" after traveling from Toronto to Tampa Bay area on May 13-14. Tried to throw off the mound on May 17 but experienced continued discomfort.

Underwent season-ending surgery on May 13. Multiple exams revealed "normal" wear and tear on labrum and femoroacetabular impingement, which limited his leg's range of motion. Expecting a six-month recovery followed by a full offseason to be ready for Spring Training.

Initially sidelined by a right shoulder injury, had his rehab assignment stalled by a sprained ankle on April 11 then shut down May 12 due to inflammation in his left shoulder. Will return to the Tampa Bay area for more imaging. No timeline for return.

A reliever for Triple-A Durham with a spot on the Rays' 40-man roster, Cook was placed on the Minor League IL on May 11. Had a 5.84 ERA with 15 strikeouts and six walks in 12 1/3 innings over 11 outings for Durham.
Signed to a Major League contract on April 14 and immediately placed on the IL. Scheduled to face hitters for the first time the weekend of May 8-10, with a rehab assignment to follow.
Did not pitch all spring while dealing with a back issue that required an injection. Also underwent an appendectomy. Will need time to build up after being shut down so long.
Sustained a Grade 2 left ankle sprain while running the bases for Triple-A Durham on April 22. Imaging revealed no fracture.

After rocky rehab outing on April 15, he reported soreness in back of right shoulder. Further evaluation revealed a new injury, a subscapularis strain. Shut down for 2-3 weeks, meaning he's likely 4-6 weeks from returning.
Underwent surgery to repair a right elbow flexor strain on Aug. 8. Hopes to return in June or July.

140.status: Out of lineup on May 25 for a second straight day with shoulder soreness. Got an MRI that came back clean. Could potentially start May 26.
Took regular batting practice on May 25 with plan to take live BP May 29. If that goes well, that’ll likely set up a rehab assignment.

140.status: Diagnosed with viral meningitis while on the IL with both a glute strain and wrist inflammation. Has returned from the hospital, but will still be away for a few more days before Rangers begin a return-to-play program.
Live batting practice scheduled for May 23 was canceled after his recovery workout didn’t go as planned. The Rangers will re-evaluate him in about a week.
Came out of the game on May 23 feeling fatigue and soreness in his shoulder, and woke up next day feeling it even more.
Had a setback in his throwing program, but began throwing from 60 feet during the Rangers’ most recent homestand and has been cleared to begin his progression again.
Reached 97 mph in one inning of a rehab appearance with Triple-A Round Rock on April 23 but experienced a setback and has been shut down.
Examined by team doctors on April 20 for shoulder soreness and was placed on the IL on April 23.
Signed to a Major League deal on Feb. 13, though is continuing to rehab from Tommy John surgery. Should be back on a big league mound around the Trade Deadline.

141.status: Placed on the IL after leaving May 24 start with left hamstring discomfort. MRI showed a mild strain, per manager John Schneider.
Out of the lineup May 25 after leaving game on May 24 when he was hit in the right elbow. Not expected to need an IL stint.
Threw two innings May 25 in first rehab game in Rookie-level Florida Complex League. Will pitch again May 31 with Single-A Dunedin.
With the Blue Jays in Toronto working through final phases of rehab before he enters rehab games.
Still a "couple of days" away from throwing.
Will start throwing again in the coming days.
Will start throwing again in the coming days.
Will throw another live BP session May 26 in Florida as he continues to ramp up slowly.

Underwent full Tommy John surgery on May 20 and will miss 12 to 18 months.
Remains in Florida. Manager John Schneider did not have an immediate update on his throwing status but mentioned he would be checking on it.
Discovered after Estrada was optioned, so he has been placed on the Major League IL.
Underwent surgery and is expected to miss the rest of 2026.
Underwent surgery to repair the labral tear and is hoping to return to the Blue Jays in five to six months. This timeline should be clearer by June, but any return in 2026 would be a success.
Underwent surgery and will miss the entire 2026 season.

142.status: Will undergo surgery on May 20, expected to miss approximately 6-8 weeks.
Initially believed to have only a muscle strain, but instead has UCL injury in thumb and will require surgery.
Has progressed to playing catch at 90 feet.
On a Minor League rehab assignment.
Played catch on May 12 and May 13, aiming to build up over next week.
Taking light swings and aiming for throwing program May 18. Will have a three-week build-up before a rehab assignment is scheduled.

142.status: Reporting improvements daily, could resume light baseball activity in a week, pending how each day goes.

143.status: Started doing "light baseball activity," per interim manager Don Mattingly, who did not clarify whether that included swinging a bat. The Phillies are being very cautious with his recovery.
Playing catch out to 105 feet. Has started the throwing program portion of building back up, per interim manager Don Mattingly.
Initially felt something in his calf while doing agility work on April 14. Began a rehab assignment on May 9, and has progressed to pitching multiple innings.
On a rehab assignment since April 30. Left Team Israel in the World Baseball Classic after feeling something in his abdomen/oblique.

144.status: Has started throwing bullpen sessions, but hasn't been cleared to begin facing hitters. He underwent surgery on June 9, 2025.
A Grade 1 strain creates hope Baldwin might be sidelined for just a month, but manager Walt Weiss said it's too early to put a timetable on the catcher's return.

Began throwing bullpens in early May. A timetable hasn’t been set, but he’s not expected to be ready until late June or early July. Underwent arthroscopic surgery on Feb. 23 to have loose bodies removed from his right elbow.
An MRI revealed a strain in Farmer’s right forearm, placing him on the 10-day injured list on May 18.

Received an encouraging update when he met with doctors in early May. But it’s too early to know when he might be cleared to begin throwing. Underwent arthroscopic surgery to remove bone spurs from his elbow on Feb. 18.
Expected to miss at least eight weeks. Was called for catcher's interference when Hyeseong Kim's bat hit his glove during May 10 game at Dodger Stadium.
Could be available at some point after the All-Star break.
Will miss the 2026 season after tearing his right ACL during a collision at first base during a Grapefruit League game on March 8.
Missed 2025 while recovering from left knee surgery and he had a cleanup procedure in November. There is no timetable for his return.

145.status: Threw on May 25, but still needs to build up arm strength.
Expected to play two more rehab games with Triple-A Charlotte before the team makes a decision on his next step.
Recently underwent an appendectomy, but has resumed throwing. The procedure did delay his rehab from Tommy John surgery, though.
Had a setback in his rehab process likely pushing him into the second half of the season. His timeline is uncertain at this point.
Hicks had felt general soreness in the area for close to two weeks. But it grew increasingly worse, so the White Sox made the move.

Sprained lateral collateral ligament in his right knee on May 16 during rehab assignment with Triple-A Charlotte. Expected to require 3-6 weeks of rest and rehabilitation before he can return to play.
First injury rehab outing with Rookie-level ACL White Sox on May 13 ended after four pitches out of the zone. Was returned from that rehab assignment due to right elbow inflammation.
White Sox Opening Day starter left his last start for Triple-A Charlotte on April 30 due to the shoulder issue. It’s the first time Smith has dealt with this malady this season.
Underwent season-ending Tommy John surgery with the procedure performed by Dr. Keith Meister, but was with the team for the home opener.
Underwent successful surgery to repair the torn ACL on Oct. 28. Estimated recovery time is eight months, per the team.

146.status: Out of May 25 lineup after HBP on elbow guard May 24. Remained in game until replaced on defense to start the sixth. Still sore but available off bench.
Dealing with a "bit of a shoulder thing," president of baseball operations Peter Bendix said to MLB Network Radio, and that it is not something that's particularly concerning.

146.injury: Left elbow UCL repair surgery with an internal brace
146.status: Underwent surgery on May 22 as recommended by Dr. Keith Meister. Recovery period calls for 10-12 months.
Exited May 20 game after taking a knee to the head. Placed on IL after experiencing concussion symptoms.
No surgery required but also no timeline for a return.

According to manager Clayton McCullough, Conine is running at 70 percent, is hitting and throwing. Trending toward a quicker timeline of return. Underwent left hamstring tendon excision week of April 13.

146.injury: UCL reconstruction with an internal brace (hybrid)
146.status: Underwent surgery, which has a recovery period of 13-14 months, on March 11. Experienced the discomfort on a pitch during his March 2 outing.

146.injury: Right elbow UCL reconstruction surgery with an associated internal brace
146.status: Underwent the hybrid procedure in December.

147.status: Has continued hitting and is performing plyometric exercises. Will undergo imaging week of May 25 with hopes of being cleared to run.

Injured shoulder crashing into wall making a catch May 7. Received PRP injection May 11. Advanced from pool and weight room activities to hitting off a tee May 20. More tee and toss on May 22. Could play Minor League games first week of June.

Shut down from throwing for a few weeks. Dr. Neal ElAttrache and team physician Dr. Chris Ahmad agreed on diagnosis and treatment, team spokesperson said May 16. Repeat imaging will be performed when asymptomatic.
Placed on IL while pitching with Triple-A Scranton/Wilkes-Barre. Will not throw for at least three weeks.

Will not throw for approximately three weeks. Manager Aaron Boone said there is “acute and chronic stuff going on there” that will require rest. Experienced difficulty while throwing on April 25 in Houston.
Threw 10 pitches from a mound April 10, his first time doing so since Tommy John surgery in July 2025. Could face hitters in June and is on track for MLB activation sometime in August.

158.status: Gave up five runs in three innings in a May 21 rehab start with Triple-A Nashville. That followed a May 16 start with High-A Wisconsin when he surrendered seven runs in 1 1/3 innings. (last updated: May 22)
Threw first live batting practice session on May 20. Decision pending as to whether he’ll now be sent on a rehab assignment. (last updated: May 22)

Simulated two innings in a 30-pitch bullpen on May 18 and was so pleased with the results that he said he is trying to push the pace of his comeback. Tentatively scheduled for three "ups" and 45-50 pitches against hitters on May 22. (last updated: May 19)

Slid into a concrete section of the wall while chasing a foul ball on May 8 against the Yankees. All of his scans came back clear for fractures and tears. Had his stitches out on May 19. (last updated: May 19)
Currently on rehab assignment with Triple-A Nashville. (last updated: May 18)
Underwent season-ending Tommy John surgery in Dallas on May 11. (last updated: May 12)
